# Hey @everyone A new Ritual Labs Research article just dropped <:pepe_pray:1213465070294536292> [**Breaking Permutation Security**](https://x.com/ritualnet/status/1927802462255337920)
## Are your prompts really that private?
The Ritual Labs Research Team has discovered a surprisingly effective attack to reverse supposedly "safe" hidden data and reconstruct what users originally typed.

## 🔍 Your private data isn't as safe as you think.
If you're using AI tools powered by large language models (like ChatGPT, Copilot, or others), there's a good chance your data is being sent to third-party inference providers. Even if they claim it's anonymized or protected through advanced methods like permuted hidden states, this research shows those protections can be broken 💥 💥 💥 

If we can’t rely on permutations for security—are we stuck with SMPC and extreme slowness for ensuring privacy? Or is there an alternative that can offer a best of both worlds?

Follow [Ritual Labs](https://x.com/ritualnet) & [Ritual Foundation](https://x.com/ritualfnd) to stay tuned.

# Hey @everyone We have an @null update straight from the @null.
##   [Cascade: Token-Sharded Private LLM Inference](https://ritual.net/blog/cascade)
In the [previous blog post by Ritual Labs](https://ritual.net/blog/breaking-permutations), the team devised a fast and effective attack, revealing that permutation-based obfuscation is not sufficient for robust security. 

So what is the solution? Token-sharding.

Cascade secures LLM inference by splitting hidden states across parties via "token sharding"—each node only sees a few tokens in sequence. The farther apart a node’s tokens are, the higher the cost of an attack—growing exponentially with distance.

The crux of Cascade is a token-sharding scheme that is robust to both our reconstruction attack and known learning-based attacks. We demonstrate in the paper that our approach, called c-δ sharding, achieves strong privacy without compromising fidelity.

Cascade’s most significant benefit is speed and scalability. Compared to existing SMPC schemes like MPCFormer and Puma, Cascade is 2 orders of magnitude faster, even the most secure setting we tested (72 participants)

Cascade was motivated by the need to find a new paradigm in the tradeoff between privacy, security and scalability. We believe its core idea—token-sharding—strikes that balance and opens a new direction for privacy-preserving LLMs.

If you're excited by what the team at Ritual Labs is researching, please join us! We are hosting a social at ICML in Vancouver. This is a great opportunity to meet our AI and crypto research team and connect with great people. A perfect Ritual.

[Register here](https://lu.ma/hj32hoa6?tk=Zo652I)
Please like & share the latest thread by Ritual Labs announcing [**Cascade**](https://x.com/ritualnet/status/1943343213236097414) <a:fire:1213442295249244232>
